summary: Justin explains how to source debt financing for multifamily deals, covering the distinction between local commercial banks for smaller loans (under $3M), loan brokers for mid-size deals, and capital advisors for larger deals ($15-20M+). He details bridge debt vs. fixed-rate debt structures, with bridge debt used for value-add renovations and fixed-rate debt for stabilized assets.
takeaways: Loans under $3 million typically come from local commercial banks in that specific market
For $10M+ acquisition loans, use loan brokers like CBRE or Bercadia, or direct relationships with Freddie/Fannie
Capital advisors enter at $15-20M+ loan sizes; they charge 3% fee on equity raised and 1% on debt sourced
You need 500+ units under ownership to work with capital advisors who will vouch for your track record
Bridge debt is used for value-add: acquisition portion + holdback for renovations, then refinance to fixed-rate debt after stabilization
Loan brokers can be found via LinkedIn and conferences; build relationships with local players before deal-hunting
Fixed-rate debt available from Freddie/Fannie or life insurance companies; bridge debt from debt funds and institutional lenders

skill_candidates.change: Develop a tiered debt sourcing strategy based on loan size thresholds
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin provides specific loan amount breakpoints: under $3M use local commercial banks, $7-10M use loan brokers, $15-20M+ use capital advisors with 500+ unit track record requirement

skill_candidates.change: Build local debt provider relationships before deal sourcing begins
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes 'getting into those markets in advance, setting up the infrastructure and the relationships is really important' so 'when you start seeing deals, you can start striking'

title: Lesson 2: The Debt
